sanskrit:
सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन् प्रकाश उपजायते।
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद् विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत॥
transliteration:
sarva-dvāreṣu dehe ’smin prakāśa upajāyate |
jñānaṃ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛddhaṃ sattvam ity uta ||
literal_meaning: When illumination (prakāśa) arises in all the gates (senses) of this body, know that the mode of goodness (sattva) has increased.
contextual_meaning: A moment of psycho-physiological coherence: every sensory doorway becomes transparent to clear perception, signaling that the mind is operating from its healthiest, least distorted register.
psychological_state: Optimal arousal zone: senses neither hyper-vigilant nor dulled, supporting integrated cognition and ethical action.
trigger_scenario: You wake up after a full night’s sleep, drink water, sit quietly for five minutes, and suddenly notice colors look richer, background noise is crisp, and you feel calmly decisive—no caffeine needed.
anchor_text: When your senses feel clear and calm, your mind is in its natural, balanced state.
